reviews.review_description:
Despite branding itself as a "Gun Simulator," this game fails to implement even the most fundamental firearm mechanisms properly. If you are a gun enthusiast who appreciates mechanical accuracy, stay away.

​1. Non-existent Slide Stop Animations
​The visual representation of the slide stop/release is completely broken. In a real firearm, when the magazine is empty, the slide stop lever moves upward and locks into the notch on the slide. In Gun Club VR, this animation is entirely omitted. The slide just hangs back in the air by "magic" without any physical interaction with the stopper lever.

​2. Blatant Modeling and Physics Errors
​The lack of attention to detail is staggering. Take the Desert Eagle for example:
​The slide stop is placed in a completely incorrect position.
​Not only is there no upward movement of the lever, but its placement is so physically impossible that it couldn't actually hold the slide back in reality—yet the slide stays open anyway.
​The Glock models suffer from similar issues, where the stopper doesn't even align with the corresponding notch on the slide.

​3. Seven Years of Neglect
​What makes this truly inexcusable is that this game has been out for over seven years. These aren't complex bugs; these are fundamental flaws in the 3D assets and animation logic. The fact that the developers have left these "rookie mistakes" unpatched for nearly a decade shows a total lack of understanding (or care) for the mechanical soul of the firearms they are trying to simulate.

​Final Verdict: This is not a simulator. It’s a poorly constructed arcade game with sloppy assets that ignore the basic laws of mechanical engineering. A huge disappointment for anyone looking for immersion.

reviews.review_title: The most realistic Gun Simulator in VR
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing this game for 2 years now, and it’s without a doubt the best gun game for VR.

First off, the graphics are really nice. Everything is so high detailed, even little things like shooting a fire extinguisher will make it leak, or shooting a brick enough times will cause it to shatter apart. Not to mention the guns themselves look really great. From the way you use them to the way they operate, it is realistic and amazing. You can do the iconic AK-47 reload where you flip the empty magazine out of the gun with the new mag! You can do bolt slaps with the MP5 and UMP45, it's little details like this that make this game great!

If I’m going to be honest, the main missions you do to buy guns and attachments aren’t very exciting. They all kinda play out the same(I’m talking about the missions for the main campaign, not the Allied, Swat, or Wild West Campaigns). There is only one reason why I have kept playing this game, and that is because of the free play mode. You get EVERYTHING for free. It’s fun to come up with all different attachment combinations and do things that are just absurd.

Of course, like everything in life, nothing is perfect. This game has flaws. Such as:
-The lack of certain guns, such as assault rifles and sniper rifles. THERE ARE OVER 20 PISTOLS LIKE GEEZ! Why isn’t there a Minigun or a BFG or an SKS?
-All the things you shoot in this game are made of cardboard. There is no 3D models of people or zombies to shoot.
-More specific gun customization. For example, in real life, you can modify the enternals of an AK-47 to make it be able to fire shotgun rounds. I’d at least like the option to do more stupid things that are completely pointless and meaningless.

Overall, I highly recommend this game. If you’re into FPS games or interested on how specific guns work, this is a great game to choose.

ALSO PLEASE MAKE MORE DLC I NEED MORE DLC I BOUGHT THE SWAT DLC AND THE WILD WEST DLC PACKS THE DAY THEY CAME OUT PLEASE

Why? Well I like the hand guns...pistols etc. So the magazine and double handed grip is the worst thing ever! So distracting you can't play. Each shooting session starts off with you chasing a dogs tail to get to your side holster to your pistol?... your body keeps rotating slightly the direction you want to get your guns so during gun range you basically turning in a circle little by little every time you start...so you have to 100% make sure of your surroundings the whole time. NOW for the double handed pistol grip...I have tried all iterations of the setup.. the only thing that mildly works is when you have your physical non dominant hand controller under your dominant grip hand...(kind of simulating real pistol hold)it is almost impossible to double grab the virtual hand onto pistol handle... 99% of the time it grips the magazine and is in this weird pose while ready to slide out mag at any moment messing up your shooting run... but then when you set it so it does not even try to grip anything...it hovers below your pistol and the stupid little fingers move around distracting the heck out of your aim...thats if the hand doesn't all of a sudden go like a meter away up in the air then behind your head and back... how the heck can one concentrate with the other hand that is supposed to lock onto the pistol grip. And if you do select grip toggle so it stays with a hold until you press again...good luck if you can get it to double hold between magazine fetch and load to holding double grip and not this little finger grip directly onto the magazine again... for goodness sakes...devs.. please let me know what to do...or then change like you have trigger option for slide grip...trigger on non dominant hand for magazine pouch mag grab and trigger for double handlhold or something. This game is almost good but this small little BIG issue is make it unplayable. Please. I cant refund but actually want to play this...but not like this. I have a ok sized youtube channel and would like to showcase this...but what the heck do I show when the non dominant hand is all over the place ?

When it works, it's alright. But at first, parts just fall off and were a pain to put back. I'd reach down to draw at the start of a round, hand slightly too close to the handgun rail apparently, so the rail pops off instantly instead of raising the gun. On restarting the round it's still missing, and to put it back on involves scrolling in the store interface to where the rail and sight attachments are. Bonkers. I've now unlocked and maximally upgraded the best guns (done in one sitting, balance is weird), still having it grab charging handle instead of the front grip, difficulty positioning hand for flashlight toggle, etc, which are not issues I've had in other VR games with detailed guns. Round summaries are regularly difficult to see because important parts are covered by a window frame, strange minor issues that individually wouldn't be a big deal, but they add up.

reviews.review_title: Paid my money then had support removed..
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game, specifically because it supported the ProtubeVR ForceTube.  It was my goto game on the quest 3.
Then after an update support for this expensive device was taken away.

Of course I  cannot refund this game after having played it for more than a year.

After being on forums, I can conclusively say that the developers of GunClubVR themselves chose to remove support on the Quest platform (could have stuck with the original ProtubveVR plugin version but erroneously chose not).  

Its very bad practice to take away something people paid for without giving the opportunity to get a refund at least.

Also - Quest 3 owners be aware that you cannot offset the controller position for yaw.  So you are literally aiming 5 degrees  left of where you will shoot when using a pistol.  As a workaround rotate the pistol away from the vertical anti clockwise.

This game has more freedom than most. Weapons have multiple contact points for various different stances. You can stick an AFG down at the end of the barrel and do a correct C grip, or put a fore grip on it and hold it there or grab it at the mag well. Unfortunately the free aim mechanics are kind of wonky and tend to bug out mid round. There’s also a hybrid stock option that locks the gun to your shoulder but I’d rather free shoot.

I find this game addicting and have probably 20 hours in it. It has some bad bugs that can be frustrating or easily exploited, if someone wants to skip the grind.
Do people not know what the refund policy is 🤣

Gun Club VR delivers one of the most enjoyable and immersive firearm simulations available in virtual reality. With an impressive variety of realistic weapons, satisfying gunplay, and a solid progression system, it’s a dream come true for VR shooting enthusiasts.

Positives:
-Weapon Variety & Realism: The game features a wide array of meticulously detailed firearms, from pistols and SMGs to assault rifles and shotguns. Handling and reloading each weapon feels great, and the attention to detail in the models and sound design really enhances immersion.
-Fun and Replayable Gameplay: Whether you’re into target shooting, zombie waves, or tactical challenges, Gun Club VR offers modes for every playstyle. It’s easy to jump into and addictive enough to keep you coming back.
-Great DLCs at a Fair Price: The extra content, including the SWAT, Wild WestDLCs, adds fresh missions and weapons, extending the game’s replayability without breaking the bank.

Areas for Improvement:
-SWAT DLC Map Design Issues: While the SWAT DLC adds a welcome tactical element, some missions place enemies or objectives in awkward locations, especially behind decorative objects. These design choices make it physically difficult to maneuver long rifles, disrupting the otherwise smooth experience.
-Glitch Report – Free Attachments Exploit: There is currently a glitch that allows players to obtain any weapon attachment for free:
	1.	Aim at the “Cancel Purchase” button.
	2.	Simultaneously press the trigger and move the analog stick (same hand) to the left.
	3.	The attachment is unlocked without payment.
While this can be tempting, it undermines the in-game progression system. It would be great to see this addressed in a future patch to maintain game balance.

Conclusion:

Gun Club VR stands out as a must-have for fans of shooting simulators in VR. It’s fun, engaging, and packed with content at a good value. While a few design hiccups and an exploit hold it back from perfection, the overall experience is still excellent. With some fixes and polish, it could easily earn a full five stars.

This appears to be a very well implemented and fun gun range app. It’s relatively easy to understand the controls, and there’s a wide variety of available weapons and scenarios. Difficulty is also very well chosen, with a genuine sense of achievement as you rank-up. Some features, like knives and grenades would benefit from better explanation, and I’ve had a couple of add ons that were difficult to remove.
Also got the SWAT and “Heavy” DLCs - the former is definitely highly recommended, but I’m finding the heavy (grenade and rocket launchers) a lot less enjoyable - e.g. some missing explosions and poor sound.
My recommendation for a new Quest 3/3S user would be to get this app and then add the SWAT DLC. I’ll get around to doing the western DLC in a week or two.

Leaning down does not move the vest down. If you temporarily exit the app, the buttons to resume the game stop working. Overall it's very cool but various usability glitches and bugs make it difficult to truly enjoy and immerse in. UPDATE: Despite the glitches, I keep coming back to it - very fun, quick gameplay. Definitely worth the money!
